supply_chain.dependencies: The Company utilizes a limited number of contract manufacturers that assemble a portion of its products. The Company purchases components from suppliers and sells those components to such contract manufacturers. The Company maintains several single-source or limited-source supplier relationships. A significant concentration of outsourced manufacturing is performed by only a few contract manufacturers, often in single locations.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to changes to raw material availability, manufacturing capacity, labor shortages, public health issues, tariffs, trade disputes and protectionist measures, extreme weather events or effects of climate change, and significant changes in the financial condition of suppliers. Single-source or limited-source suppliers may adversely affect the availability or timely delivery of critical products or components. Concentration risk with contract manufacturers. Elevated backlog levels for AI-optimized servers due to GPU supply constraints.

regulatory_risks: Antitrust regulatory activities of the U.S. Federal Trade Commission, U.S. Department of Justice, and European Union
Consumer protection laws and financial services regulation
Export regulatory activities of the U.S. Department of Commerce and U.S. Department of the Treasury
Import regulatory activities of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection
Product safety regulatory activities of the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission and U.S. Department of Transportation
Health information privacy and security requirements of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
Environmental, employment and labor regulations in various countries

Product design and procurement regulations relating to materials composition, sourcing, radiated emissions, energy efficiency, collection, recycling, treatment, transportation, and disposal of electronics products
Restrictions on mercury, lead, cadmium, lithium metal, lithium ion, and other substances
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and other anti-corruption laws
Export controls, customs regulations, economic sanctions laws and embargoes
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act provisions on conflict minerals
EU Artificial Intelligence Act and other AI regulations
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax
Trade protection measures, tariffs, and trade barriers

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions laws and embargoes imposed by the U.S. government
Violations of export controls, customs regulations, or economic sanctions laws may result in severe criminal or civil sanctions and penalties
Trade protection measures and disputes could result in increased tariffs and other trade restrictions
